checks.a_analysis: The output includes a section titled '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]' which acts as a status note for the conversion failure. While this text is not in the source, it falls under the category of pipeline-added metadata/labels for long or problematic sections and is not fabricated 'content' intended to mislead. The rest of the text appears derived from the source.
The contact number 1-800-858-0368 and the address for the Division of Water Resources are correctly extracted from pages 11 and 12 of the source document.

The source document has 26 pages. The conversion results start from page 10 (item 9.d) and continue through the end, but completely omit Pages 1-9. This includes Section I (Performance Standards), Section II (Operation and Maintenance Requirements), and a large portion of Section III. The output explicitly contains a note stating pages 1-11 are unavailable.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains pages 1-10 including Section I (Performance Standards) and Section II (Operation and Maintenance Requirements).
checks.b_evidence_output: [Pages 1-11 unavailable] ... Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted.
checks.b_evidence_location: Beginning of document to Section IV (Page 14 in source)

checks.a_analysis: The 'Attachment B: Groundwater Monitoring' table on page 25 of the source document contains 9 rows of parameters. The corresponding table in the output only contains 1 row (Chloride), missing 89% of the table data.
checks.b_evidence_source: Main table in Attachment B contains 9 rows: Chloride, Coliform, Nitrogen Ammonia, Nitrogen Nitrate, pH, Phosphorus, Sodium, Solids, Water Level.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table under 'Attachment B: Groundwater Monitoring' contains only the 'Chloride (as Cl)' row.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Section 'VI. PENALTIES' is present as a heading in the output, but its elements list is empty. In the source document, this section contains three substantial numbered paragraphs.
checks.b_evidence_source: Section VI. PENALTIES: 1. Failure to abide by the conditions... 2. The Permittee must comply... 3. It shall not be a defense...

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There is a discrepancy in a regulatory citation. The source document cites [15A NCAC 02T .0105(m)] for a requirement regarding monitoring results, whereas the output cites [15A NCAC 02T .0108]. This alters the legal reference.
checks.b_evidence_source: results below applicable groundwater standards. – [15A NCAC 02T .0105(m)]
checks.b_evidence_output: results below applicable groundwater standards. – [15A NCAC 02T .0108]
checks.b_evidence_location: Section III, Item 11 continuation paragraph, Page 11

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document. While there are significant omissions and misorderings, no specific text blocks appear more times in the output than they do in the source PDF.

The heading hierarchy is severely broken. Section VI ('PENALTIES') is identified as a heading in Section index 4, but its content is missing from that section and instead merged into the list items of Section IV (Section index 2). This misassignment of content to headings violates the structural organization of the document.

Table 3 in Section 8 ('Groundwater Monitoring Requirements') is structurally corrupted. It correctly identifies the headers but fails to capture 9 out of the 10 data rows present in the source table on Page 25. Only the row for 'Chloride (as Cl)' is present; parameters like 'Coliform', 'Nitrogen', 'pH', etc., are lost, making the table structure incomplete and unfaithful to the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Groundwater Monitoring Requirements table on Page 25 containing 10 rows of data (Chloride through Water Level).
checks.b_evidence_output: Table 3 in Section 8 contains only one data row for 'Chloride (as Cl)'.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is fundamentally compromised. First, the document skips the first 10 pages entirely, beginning at Page 11. Second, the content of Pages 11 through 14 is flattened into a single list in Section 1, losing section boundaries. Third, items from Section V (General Conditions) and Section VI (Penalties) are incorrectly inserted into the list for Section IV (Inspections and Entry) in Section index 2. This creates a logical flow that does not exist in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document begins on Page 1 with the permit title and criteria. Sections V and VI are distinct sections following Section IV.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON begins at Page 11 content. Section 2 contains list items from sections IV, V, and VI merged together.

checks.a_analysis: Lists are converted incorrectly throughout the first half of the output. In Section 1, list items from multiple pages (11-14) are merged into one flat list. In Section 2, items that are top-level section headings or independent list items in the source (V.1, V.2, VI.1, VI.2, VI.3) are converted as child items of Section IV's list.
checks.b_evidence_source: Distinct lists for Section IV (item 1 with sub-items a-d), Section V (items 1, 2), and Section VI (items 1, 2, 3).
checks.b_evidence_output: A single list in Section 2 containing IV.1 (a-d), followed by 1, 2 (from Section V) and 1, 2, 3 (from Section VI).

checks.a_analysis: There are no links present in the JSON. While the source contains a URL (www.weather.gov on page 7), that page was missed by the conversion. No fabricated links were added.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output has two significant layout differences from the source. First, the 4-column list of animal types on page 22 of the PDF is collapsed into a single, continuous text block in the HTML, which makes the categorization (Swine, Cattle, Dry Poultry, etc.) difficult to distinguish and confuses the meaning. Second, there is a false error message at the bottom of the HTML output stating '[Pages 1-11 unavailable] Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted', despite that content being correctly rendered earlier in the document. This is a major organizational failure that misleads the user about the document's integrity.
checks.b_evidence_source: The PDF (page 22) lists animal types in four distinct columns with headers: Swine, Cattle, Dry Poultry, and Other Types.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows this content as a single block of text: 'Swine Wean to Finish Wean to Feeder... Dry Poultry... Wet Poultry... Other Types...'. Also, the text: '[Pages 1-11 unavailable] Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted.'
checks.b_evidence_location: Attachment A, following the Production Summary table and at the very bottom of the HTML document.

checks.a_analysis: The table in Attachment B (page 25 of the source PDF) is misrendered in the HTML. The source table features primary grouping headers ('GROUNDWATER CHARACTERISTICS', 'GROUNDWATER STANDARDS', and 'MONITORING REQUIREMENTS') that span multiple columns. In the HTML output, these grouping headers are completely missing, leaving only the secondary column headers. This loss of structural hierarchy significantly degrades the readability and understanding of the table's data categories.
checks.b_evidence_source: Attachment B: Groundwater Monitoring table (page 25) with top-level grouping headers: 'GROUNDWATER CHARACTERISTICS', 'GROUNDWATER STANDARDS', 'MONITORING REQUIREMENTS'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table for Attachment B only shows the bottom-level headers: 'PCS Code', 'Parameter Description', 'Daily Maximum', 'Frequency Measurement', 'Sample Type', 'Footnotes'.

checks.a_analysis: The handwritten signature of Linda Culpepper, Director, which appears on page 19 of the source PDF, is missing from the HTML output. For a formal government permit, the signature is a meaningful visual element indicating authorization and validity. Its absence makes the converted document appear incomplete or unofficial.
checks.b_evidence_source: Image of handwritten signature above the name 'Linda Culpepper' on page 19.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML version only shows the text 'Linda Culpepper' with no corresponding signature image.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 19, end of Section V. General Conditions.

checks.a_analysis: No body content (paragraphs, specific table rows) appears to be duplicated in the rendered HTML. While document identifiers like 'Permit Number AWG300000' appear multiple times at section breaks, this is an appropriate structural adaptation for a continuous-scroll HTML document representing multiple pages with headers.
